description: Developing a quick feedback system for making confident code commits and reducing defect leakage are the key business problems we are trying to solve. Sahi helps as it has fast development cycle, easy to setup framework and strong fault-tolerant recognition capabilities.
author: Ashwini L.
pros: Test automation ease is based on out of the box framework components like fault tolerant recognition parameters, reporting and exception handling . Relational operators in sahi provide easy to write DOM recognition parameters. Moreover these are not brittle like XPATH recognition strings. Reporting and exception handling is built into the framework. That provides a quick system in place to start test automation with minimal setup. Unlike other frameworks where in various components need to be in place before one can actually start putting in test automation. ROI in test automation frameworks is long term. And it is realized only when automated tests start catching bugs. Initial framework building time gets cuts down in case of SAHI and hence time for ROI realization gets cut down.
cons: Maintenance is a big overhead for any test automation project. Hence debugging capabilities greatly help cut down maintenance effort. There is scope for improvement in case of debugging capabilities. Here are some areas which can be improved in the software. (1) For large scale test automation, amount of time it takes to analyze a failing script and conclude if it is a false alarm can be cut down. Common interaction/recognition failures can be rolled up at suite level. This will help summarize total failing constructs. This debugging info is present at the script level today (2) Just like snapshot capturing, if there can be video capturing of playback, it will greatly help understanding behavior of application under test, test environment and the test automation tool when put for un-attended schedules. One can still setup a desktop recorder in absence of the same. But a tool capability provides way to turn on such debugging only in cases where it is not easy to debug why certain scripts fail in an un-attended run. (3) Heuristic analysis on consistent failing scripts, suites and similar insights should provide quick correction mechanism for reducing waste in the system. There is already suite level playback information stored in the SAHI DB. That data can be analyzed for heuristic/predictive analysis providing recommendations and corrections in overall test automation system. Currently there is only suite level pass/fail information.
